title: Async write-lock leaks open transaction if COMMIT throws

findingDetails.evidence: In runWithWriteLock the promise success handler runs database.exec('COMMIT') unguarded (lines 210-213); a COMMIT failure rejects the promise with no ROLLBACK. The synchronous branch (line 221) is inside the outer try and rolls back via catch→rollbackQuietly (lines 223-226). Confirmed dormant: grep shows withWriteLock has no production callers and is only invoked with sync operations in two tests, so the promise branch is never exercised.
findingDetails.why: Asymmetric error handling: a leaked open transaction on the shared connection makes subsequent withWriteLock calls see database.inTransaction===true and silently fall back to withRetry without the write lock (lines 94-99), running operations inside the leaked transaction. A correctness/durability gap at the contention port boundary.
findingDetails.recommendation: Wrap the async COMMIT in try/catch and call rollbackQuietly before rethrowing, mirroring the synchronous path: change the .then success handler to { try { database.exec('COMMIT'); return value; } catch (e) { rollbackQuietly(database); throw e; } }.

title: VectorStore.clear() orphans per-embedder shard table under non-default embedder

findingDetails.evidence: clear() deletes only vec_memories, active_memory_projection, and memory_index (lines 307-311). But writeActiveVectorPayload also inserts the embedding into a per-dim shard vecTableNameForDim when a non-default embedder is active (vector-index-mutations.ts:87-90), and the per-record deleteActiveVectorPayload removes that shard row (mutations.ts:95-98). clear() never touches the shard. activeVectorSource('vec_memories') only schema-qualifies the name (vector-index-store.ts:456-458); it does not redirect to the shard.
findingDetails.why: Contradicts clear()'s own JSDoc ('Full memory-store reset … removes all vector records AND the associated projection and index metadata'): under a non-default embedder the dim-specific shard retains orphaned vectors after a full reset, inconsistent with single-record deletion which cleans the shard. Dormant today (no production caller of the port clear() and default embedder leaves dimSource null).
findingDetails.recommendation: In clear(), also delete from the active dim shard, reusing the same resolution as deleteActiveVectorPayload (getActiveEmbedder/vecTableNameForDim), e.g. add a DELETE for activeDimVectorSource(database) inside the transaction when it is non-null.
